CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Third Amended Complaint Filed By Plaintiffs Whitney Clarke And Aida Jones
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Continued To Jun-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice By Defendants Lux Leasing, Llc, Loop Transportation, Inc., And Wedriveu, Inc. Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Complaint Filed By Plaintiffs Whitney Clarke And Aida Jones
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JOSHUA C. EZRIN, ESQ. and LING KUANG, ESQ. of Audet & PArnters, LLP, Tel No. 415-568-2555 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. MATTHEW ROBERTS, ESQ. of Bishop / Barry, Tel No. 510-420-6339 appearing for Defendants Lux Leasing, LLC, Loop Transporation, Inc. and Wedriveu, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to Defendants Lux Leasing, LLC, Loop Transportation, Inc., and Wedriveu, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Complaint Filed By Plaintiffs Whitney Clarke And Aida Jones, Defendants' motion to strike the third cause of action by plaintiff Whitney Clarke is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer to that claim and their motion to strike the third prayer of relief is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiffs' third prayer seeks injunctive relief that far exceeds the scope of the alleged unlawful conduct by defendants and is so overbroad as to make it impossible or virtually impossible for defendants to comply absent defendants ceasing their shuttle operations in all of San Francisco. Plaintiffs are given another opportunity to allege entitlement to injunctive relief that would remedy the alleged harm to their rights yet not be so broad as be tantamount to a city-wide ban on defendants' shuttles. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JOSHUA C. EZRIN, ESQ. and LING KUANG, ESQ. of Audet & PArnters, LLP, Tel No. 415-568-2555 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. MATTHEW ROBERTS, ESQ. of Bishop / Barry, Tel No. 510-420-6339 appearing for Defendants Lux Leasing, LLC, Loop Transporation, Inc. and Wedriveu, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS LUX LEASING, LLC, LOOP TRANSPORTATION, INC., AND WEDRIVEU, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action by plaintiff Whitney Clarke is sustained without leave to amend. In the order filed August 12, 2015 Judge Goldsmith rejected the argument that diminution of the value of a leasehold is sufficient to satisfy the standing requirements of Business and Professions code section 17204. Mr. Clarke has not shown that Judge Goldsmith's ruling is incorrect. Mr. Clarke's reliance upon Daro v. Superior Court (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 1079, 1090 is misplaced. In Daro the trial court noted that the tenants had UCL standing by virtue of the threatened loss of their leasehold interests. The loss of their leasehold interests was based on the fact that they were being evicted. Here, Mr. Clarke is not being evicted, instead he alleges that the lease is less valuable because of defendants' actions and he is losing money because the rent he pays does not reflect the decreased value of the lease. Although Law Offices of Matthew Higbee v. Expungement Assistance Services (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 544 noted that the moving party had UCL standing based in part on the diminished value of its business, the diminished value of the business was not the only source of the moving party's economic injury. The moving party had also alleged that it had experienced economic injury because it was forced to pay increased advertising costs and had to reduce prices for services in order to compete. Mr. Clarke has not alleged any other economic injury aside from the diminished value of his leasehold interest and he has not cited case law which supports the position that a tenant possesses UCL standing based solely on a diminution of the leasehold's value. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice By Defendants Lux Leasing, Llc, Loop Transportation, Inc., And Wedriveu, Inc. Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Complaint Filed By Plaintiffs Whitney Clarke And Aida Jones
